data.four_set_effect: When Normal Attacks hit opponents, there is a 36% chance that it will trigger Valley Rite, which will increase Normal Attack DMG by 70% of ATK. This effect will be dispelled 0.05s after a Normal Attack deals DMG. If a Normal Attack fails to trigger Valley Rite, the odds of it triggering the next time will increase by 20%. This trigger can occur once every 0.2s.

circlet_of_logos.desc: A pair of earrings made from a single piece of jade. It has a most gentle texture.
circlet_of_logos.title: Flowing Rings

flower_of_life.desc: A jade carved into the shape of a flower. A phantom scent, here one instant and gone the next, swirls around it.
flower_of_life.title: Soulscent Bloom

goblet_of_eonothem.desc: This teacup forever overflows with fresh water. Perhaps it was a gift from an adeptus, one of their relics, or just something they left behind.
goblet_of_eonothem.title: Chalice of the Font

plume_of_death.desc: A jade ornament shaped like a leaf. It seems to have once had deep meaning between specific friends.
plume_of_death.title: Jade Leaf

sands_of_eon.desc: A circular jade ornament. Legend has it that it was once used somewhere as a symbol for rituals to begin.
sands_of_eon.title: Symbol of Felicitation

list.desc: Each year, when the Spiritscent Flowers bloom, Qiaoying Village will prepare for its tea-serving ceremonies.</br>Once the flowers wither, flower tea infused with nine layers of scents are presented in the village hall.</br>The Spiritscent Flower is a fleeting thing, much like the sudden coming and going of a certain adeptus.</br>This one left behind the ambiguous name of "Herblord," as well as many foggy and fragmented legends.</br>In one such tale, the Herblord's adeptal form would turn into the branches of an ancient tea tree.</br>In another, the adeptus flew up to the adeptal mountain on a subdued evil beast.</br>There is even a story that goes like this—</br>The young lady grasped at the hooded hat on the ground as she struggled ashore, placing it on her head haphazardly.</br>For if she did not cover her face, then she might feel embarrassed and find herself at a loss for words.</br>Just then, the orchestrator of her suffering poked their head out of the water.</br>The rainbow-scales on their body glimmered, as if to revel in this victory.</br>"*cough* Alright! So you can swim! Very impressive! ...You know what? May you drown someday!"</br>She spoke these words in anger, but also in jest.</br>And yet that glittering streak would eventually sink into the depths, never to come back to the surface.</br>
list.title: Soulscent Bloom

list.desc: Chenyu Vale is home to many mountains, streams, and stories. Among these, the most famous is:</br>A long time ago, there was a priceless gem that fell into the hands of a demon and was thrown into the waters where it sunk...</br>The wide river of a legend will oft produce many tributaries. One of these goes like this:</br>This gem was once jade from a sacred mountain, carved into its current shape only by the hand of Rex Lapis himself.</br>And the stone that had been lost to the waters may have been a lesser jade, or perhaps just a simple cup.</br>Some even say that the "jade" in the tale is in fact an analogy for a beautiful person.</br>The legends even have it that people had once seen this:</br>Unnumbered koi with tails like gems in the sunlight,</br>Having left the shackles of the lakes and rivers to which aquatic creatures are bound,</br>Flying freely with the wind in the sky.</br>So too did the pair of jade earrings belonging to a certain person change form.</br>
list.title: Flowing Rings

list.desc: This was originally a gift from a friend, linked to a small realm within.</br>The spring within the chalice would never dry up, making it a fine place to temporarily stay.</br>It could hold a reflection of the sun and moon, and could play host to swimming fish.</br>Compared to the ill-fate accorded to the Yaksha, she believed that she was luckier by far.</br>But the price for inheriting the ancient rites was to never spend much time on land.</br>In those days, the sweet waters did not flow across Liyue in such abundance.</br>The harbor-city beneath the mountains and the gathering in the plains was, to her, a distant dream.</br>But that person, ever afraid of trouble, decided to set off with this teacup in hand.</br>This "Liyue Harbor" she spoke of would almost certainly be as full of flaws as a village ceremony.</br>This journey would surely be full of arguments, struggle, and many troubles.</br>She knew that they both quite enamored with their gifts of the gab, but much less with crowds.</br>Nowhere else in this world could you find two adepti as small as they, envious and fearing prosperity in equal measure.</br>"But we had promised each other many things in the past, and this is very good."</br>As they were to leave, she thought thus.</br>"Well, at least this trip will be interesting. I can introduce her to some other old friends."</br>Later, the brazier and tea kettle would come into common use, and the teacup's shape, too, was taken up by people.</br>And thus did everyone become able to have the moon on their desk and hold it in their palms.</br>
list.title: Chalice of the Font

list.desc: A very long time ago, there was no ford across the river, only a misty hillside.</br>The owner of this mountain had yet to decide what to plant here when someone preempted them.</br>"Once this tree gets a bit larger, I'll cut its leaves down and make some tea for all of you."</br>"When that time comes, we'll get Cloud Retainer and Mountain Shaper to come over..."</br>"Seriously? You plant trees any way you like on my turf, and you have the nerve to spout such things?"</br>But though the young lady who was master of this mountain complained, she too could imagine the tea's fragrance.</br>Later, someone would quietly tie this jade pendant to the thin branches of the small tree.</br>When more time had passed, the mountain's master returned, but in a changed aspect,</br>And sans the finger that might untie the pendant. This was a long time ago now.</br>Many years later, the branches of this tree would be grafted to the other side of the river by the mortals who dwelled in the mountains.</br>The fragrance of tea would also travel from this place, Chenyu Vale, to Liyue Harbor, and from there to many more places still.</br>There are many legends concerning the tea trees of Chenyu Vale. One of them goes like this:</br>No matter the waters, soil, or sunlight, this sort of tree only grows lushly in Chenyu Vale.</br>This is because it remembers the promise that old friends made in the ancient past beside the tea sapling.</br>
list.title: Jade Leaf

list.desc: It is said that this jade ornament comes from the long-sealed sacred mountain.</br>Just as a Starconch separated from the sea will recall the sound of the waves,</br>The ornament will also emit the sound of flowing water.</br>You may often hear such rumors in inns...</br>"You know, legend has it that the greatest treasure of the mountains is a slab of fine jade that can bring down sweet rains."</br>"But when the world was in turmoil, demons began to covet its power."</br>"So the master of the mountain split it into many parts, forming each part into different shapes, so as to hide them."</br>"Then, the master concealed them underwater, in the hills, and some were even offered to shrines."</br>"In the legends of Chenyu Vale, these jade ornaments bear the blessing of a deity's pact."</br>"It's just that no one has been able to find them, even after so many years..."</br>The priest had been ever careful in hiding this jade loop on their person.</br>But on certain years, they would discreetly show it to a friend with no sense of refinement on the eve of a departure.</br>The priest would speak of its patterns and its origins, and of the pact made with a deity by their ancestors,</br>But the friend was busy pounding herbs with mortar and pestle, and did not hear a single word.</br>"We do this song and dance every year, and I've heard this story who-knows-how-many times."</br>"Didn't you say you'd treat me to some tea when you get back? Let's talk then."</br>But that which came from the waters was not that which she had thought would come, and would vanish henceforth back into those waters...</br>Till today, the artisans of Yilong Port still make such simple ornaments.</br>Visiting merchants will also often place these jade loops close to their ears, as the legend says,</br>Wondering if they can truly hear the sound of the rain pitter-pattering on mountain stone.</br>
list.title: Symbol of Felicitation
